title: cornus alba subsp. tatarica
intro: Cornus alba subsp. tatarica, also known as the Red-osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to Northern Asia and Eastern Europe, characterized by its vibrant red stems and white flowers. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, leaves, and roots, which contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming Cornus alba subsp. tatarica has been linked to various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in weight loss, due to its high levels of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The herbal preparations of Cornus alba subsp. tatarica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from fever and sore throats to digestive issues and skin conditions.
